method_atom: A technique for computing the multi-point correlation functions of a stochastic ensemble in closed form by expressing them as the Pfaffian of an antisymmetric matrix built from a 2x2 matrix-valued kernel, and then extracting universal large-size asymptotics by taking scaling limits of these correlations both in the interior (bulk) and near the boundary (edge) of the support.
object_atom: The real Ginibre ensemble: a class of large asymmetric real random matrices and the statistical distribution of their (real and complex-conjugate) eigenvalues.
brief_reason: The Pfaffian-kernel correlation-function machinery and its bulk/edge scaling-limit analysis carry across as domain-neutral tools, while the asymmetric-real-random-matrix eigenvalue ensemble is the random-matrix-theory-specific object they act on.
